en.body:
# Capability Productization Cycle

A cycle for turning business capabilities into reusable digital capabilities before selecting the implementation style.

Purpose: Identify and productize reusable digital capabilities before deciding whether the delivery style should be APIs, events, files, streams, data products, AI-enabled services, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.
Audience: business-owner, platform-architect, capability-owner

## Route

Strategy -> Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Architecture & Platform Decisions -> Solution & Interface Design -> Delivery & Operations -> Quality & Readiness Assurance -> Publishing & Enablement -> Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Customer Discovers Need | How does the customer recognize their need or problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Who is the typical customer experiencing this journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Capability Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Consumer Requirements & Onboarding - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Architecture & Platform Decisions - Business Impact Canvas

Purpose: What are the potential business impacts if the capability, integration, or service fails?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Availability Risks | What are the potential risks to capability availability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Security Risks | What are the potential security risks associated with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Data Risks | What are the potential risks to data integrity or confidentiality? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Availability Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the availability risks? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Security Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the security risks? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Data Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the data risks? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Architecture & Platform Decisions - Location Canvas

Purpose: What geopolitical, regulatory, network, and trust boundaries affect this capability or integration?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Location / Trust Groups | What are the relevant geopolitical, regulatory, network, or trust groups? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Group Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those groups, such as residency, trust level, or network exposure? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Relevant Locations / Zones | What are the relevant locations, zones, or environments within each group? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Location / Zone Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those locations or zones, such as ownership, region, or exposure? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Network / Regulatory Distances | What latency, trust, regulatory, or connectivity distances exist between the locations? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Distance Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those distances, such as latency sensitivity, residency constraints, or trust boundaries? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Connectivity Endpoints | What connectivity endpoints or interfaces are associated with the locations? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Endpoint Access Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those endpoints, such as exposure, protocol, security, or access restrictions? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |

### Architecture & Platform Decisions - Capacity Canvas

Purpose: How much capacity is needed to support expected capability consumption?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Current Business Volumes | What are the current business volumes and transaction rates? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Future Consumption Trends | What are the anticipated future consumption trends? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Peak Load and Availability Requirements | What are the peak load and availability requirements? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Caching Strategies | What caching strategies can be used to optimize performance? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Rate Limiting Strategies | What rate limiting strategies can be used to manage consumption? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Scaling Strategies | What scaling strategies can be used to accommodate growth? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |

en.body:
# API Productization Cycle

The API-focused APIOps Cycles journey for productizing, designing, delivering, publishing, and improving APIs.

Purpose: Preserve the current API-focused method journey for teams that already know the intended delivery style is an API product.
Audience: api-product-owner, api-designer, api-architect

## Route

API Product Strategy -> API Consumer Experience -> API Platform Architecture -> API Design -> API Delivery -> API Audit -> API Publishing -> Monitoring & Improving

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### API Product Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Customer Discovers Need | How does the customer recognize their need or problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Who is the typical customer experiencing this journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### API Product Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### API Product Strategy - API Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: How does the customer journey map to APIs? What end-user and API consumer pains, and gains need to be addressed?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=apiValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tasks | What are the customers (end-users) trying to achieve? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain Enabling Features | What features enable end-users and API consumers to achieve gains? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain Relieving Features | What features help end-users and  API consumers overcome pains? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| API Products | What API products and features address the tasks, pains, and gains? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |

### API Product Strategy - API Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How feasible and reusable will this API be? Do we have a business case from a cost - benefit point of view?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=apiBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Key Partners | Who are the key stakeholders involved? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key Activities | What are the most important actions the API provider must take to operate successfully? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key Resources | What unique strategic assets must the API provider acquire or build? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| API Value Proposition | Start with one sticky note naming the API or API family, then capture what value the API offers to API consumers. | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Developer Relations | How does the API provider reach and support API consumers? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which mechanisms do API consumers interact with the API? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |
| API Consumer Segments | Who are the target audiences for the API? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |

en.body:
h1. API Productization Cycle

The API-focused APIOps Cycles journey for productizing, designing, delivering, publishing, and improving APIs.

Purpose: Preserve the current API-focused method journey for teams that already know the intended delivery style is an API product.
Audience: api-product-owner, api-designer, api-architect

h2. Route

API Product Strategy -> API Consumer Experience -> API Platform Architecture -> API Design -> API Delivery -> API Audit -> API Publishing -> Monitoring & Improving

h2. Intent & Fit

Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

h2. Requirements

Gather expert input using the recommended questions. Treat answers as inputs to design decisions, not final design decisions.

h2. API Architecture Decision

Document API architecture, platform, security, lifecycle, and publishing decisions.

h2. Architecture Decision Record

Record the selected decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences, evidence, and follow-up actions.

h2. Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

h3. API Product Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||

| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. API Product Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

h3. API Product Strategy - API Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: How does the customer journey map to APIs? What end-user and API consumer pains, and gains need to be addressed?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=apiValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Tasks | What are the customers (end-users) trying to achieve? | api-product-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. API Product Strategy - API Business Model Canvas

en.body:
# Integration Productization Cycle

A cycle for productizing reusable integration capabilities before selecting a concrete integration pattern.

Purpose: Guide reusable integration capability design before choosing APIs, events, files, streams, data products, direct integration, or hybrid patterns.
Audience: platform-architect, integration-architect, platform-owner

## Route

Integration Capability Strategy -> Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Integration Architecture Decision -> Integration Solution Design -> Integration Delivery & Operations -> Integration Readiness Assurance -> Capability Publishing -> Integration Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Integration Capability Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Customer Discovers Need | How does the customer recognize their need or problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Who is the typical customer experiencing this journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Architecture Decision - Business Impact Canvas

Purpose: What are the potential business impacts if the capability, integration, or service fails?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Availability Risks | What are the potential risks to capability availability? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Security Risks | What are the potential security risks associated with the capability? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Data Risks | What are the potential risks to data integrity or confidentiality? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Availability Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the availability risks? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Security Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the security risks? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Mitigate Data Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the data risks? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Architecture Decision - Location Canvas

Purpose: What geopolitical, regulatory, network, and trust boundaries affect this capability or integration?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Location / Trust Groups | What are the relevant geopolitical, regulatory, network, or trust groups? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Group Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those groups, such as residency, trust level, or network exposure? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Relevant Locations / Zones | What are the relevant locations, zones, or environments within each group? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Location / Zone Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those locations or zones, such as ownership, region, or exposure? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Network / Regulatory Distances | What latency, trust, regulatory, or connectivity distances exist between the locations? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Distance Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those distances, such as latency sensitivity, residency constraints, or trust boundaries? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Connectivity Endpoints | What connectivity endpoints or interfaces are associated with the locations? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Endpoint Access Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those endpoints, such as exposure, protocol, security, or access restrictions? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Architecture Decision - Capacity Canvas

Purpose: How much capacity is needed to support expected capability consumption?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Current Business Volumes | What are the current business volumes and transaction rates? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Future Consumption Trends | What are the anticipated future consumption trends? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Peak Load and Availability Requirements | What are the peak load and availability requirements? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Caching Strategies | What caching strategies can be used to optimize performance? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Rate Limiting Strategies | What rate limiting strategies can be used to manage consumption? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Scaling Strategies | What scaling strategies can be used to accommodate growth? | platform-owner |  |  |  |  |

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Availability Risks | What are the potential risks to capability availability? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Security Risks | What are the potential security risks associated with the capability? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Data Risks | What are the potential risks to data integrity or confidentiality? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |

en.body:
Use this page to gather technology-agnostic integration requirements before deciding whether the solution should be an API, event, file exchange, stream, data product, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.

Intent & fit needs
Purpose: Quickly capture the integration's job-to-be-done and key context.

| Field | Value |
| Integration / capability name |  |
| Job to be done | What business outcome must be achieved? |
| Producer / source systems |  |
| Consumer / target systems |  |
| Core business nouns | Order, Product, Customer... |
| Trigger / event | What initiates the process? |
| Non-functional drivers | Latency, availability, privacy, regulatory, volume, recovery |
| Scope / out of scope |  |
| Decision status | Requirements gathering |

Method path

Line: Core Capability Method
Path: Strategy -> Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Architecture & Platform Decisions -> Solution & Interface Design -> Delivery & Operations -> Quality & Readiness Assurance -> Publishing & Enablement -> Monitoring & Improvement

Text sources
Integration document canvas order and station grouping: src/data/method/integration-extension.json
Integration line and station descriptions: src/data/method/lines.json, src/data/method/stations.json, and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.*.json
Canvas title, purpose, and questions: src/data/canvas/localizedData.json
Station descriptions and instructions: src/data/method/stations.json and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.stations.json
Example answers, when available: src/data/canvas/import-export-templates/*.json

Business flow visualization
Add a simple process outline or Mermaid diagram showing producer, consumer, trigger, data movement, and key decision points.

Requirements

Strategy
Station description: Frame the business need as a reusable capability before choosing the implementation style.
Why it matters: Integration and API work often jumps too quickly to a technical pattern. This station keeps the team focused on the business journey, domain meaning, value, reuse potential, ownership, and viability before selecting APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration.

Customer Journey Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
Station instruction: Map the customer or partner journey that creates the capability need and reveals tasks, pains, gains, inputs, outputs, and decision points.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Customer Discovers Need | How does the customer recognize their need or problem? | _Example: Need to find the right product_ |
| Persona | Who is the typical customer experiencing this journey? | _Example: Mobile shopper comparing options; Wants quick, confident choice_ |
| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | _Example: Too many irrelevant results; Filters are hard to understand; Price or stock looks stale_ |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | _Example: Find the right product fast_ |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | _Example: Relevant results quickly; Easy comparison on mobile; Confident variant choice_ |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | _Example: Inputs: need, keyword, filters; Outputs: options, detail, choice_ |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | _Example: Show relevant results first; Keep filters simple; Hide unavailable options_ |

Domain Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
Station instruction: Define the core entities, attributes, relationships, ownership, and business rules that the capability must respect.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | _Example: Product: merchandised offering; Variant: purchasable option; Category: browse grouping; SearchQuery: shopper intent; Facet: filterable dimension_ |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | _Example: Product: id, slug, name, status; Variant: sku, options, active; Category: id, name, parent; SearchQuery: term, filters, market; Facet: name, values, counts_ |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | _Example: SearchQuery returns Products; Product has many Variants; Product belongs to Categories; Facet refines SearchQuery; Variant belongs to Product_ |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | _Example: Only published Products appear; Only active Variants selectable; Product ids stay stable; Search ranking should be explainable_ |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | _Example: Catalog data is public read; No PII in SearchQuery; Internal fields stay hidden; Admin writes stay separate_ |

Capability Value Proposition Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
Station instruction: Use the Capability Value Proposition Canvas to capture consumer tasks, gains, pains, and reusable capability features without naming the delivery technology too early.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? |  |

Capability Business Model Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
Station instruction: Use the Capability Business Model Canvas to clarify ownership, partners, channels, costs, benefits, support, and lifecycle expectations for the reusable capability.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? |  |

Consumer Requirements & Onboarding
Station description: Capture consumer onboarding, standards, non-functional requirements, service expectations, constraints, security needs, allowed protocols, data freshness, SLAs, observability, recovery, adoption requirements, and producer responsibilities.
Why it matters: The right architecture depends on consumer goals, onboarding expectations, service levels, data quality needs, change tolerance, observability, support, and producer constraints.

Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
Station instruction: Use the Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas to capture consumer goals, availability, freshness, volume, performance, data quality, security, onboarding, change, observability, and recovery expectations.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? |  |

Architecture & Platform Decisions
Station description: Use requirements and constraints to decide the right architecture pattern and enabling platform capabilities.
Why it matters: Architecture choices should follow from evidence about business impact, locations, trust boundaries, capacity, latency, data ownership, consistency, operability, security, privacy, governance, and cost.

Business Impact Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What are the potential business impacts if the capability, integration, or service fails?
Station instruction: Use the Business Impact Canvas to identify availability, security, and data risks that influence architecture options.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Availability Risks | What are the potential risks to capability availability? | _Example: Search outage blocks discovery; Traffic spikes overload search; Slow responses hurt UX_ |
| Security Risks | What are the potential security risks associated with the capability? | _Example: Public API abused by bots; Internal fields exposed; Query logs leak user intent_ |
| Data Risks | What are the potential risks to data integrity or confidentiality? | _Example: Stale index shows wrong results; Hidden products appear in results; Inconsistent IDs break linking_ |
| Mitigate Availability Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the availability risks? | _Example: Cache popular queries; Autoscale search services; Graceful fallback results_ |
| Mitigate Security Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the security risks? | _Example: Rate limit public access; Filter response fields; Sanitize logs and queries_ |
| Mitigate Data Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the data risks? | _Example: Validate indexed data; Enforce visibility rules; Monitor index freshness_ |

Locations Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What geopolitical, regulatory, network, and trust boundaries affect this capability or integration?
Station instruction: Use the Locations Canvas to capture geopolitical, regulatory, network, residency, and trust-boundary constraints.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Location / Trust Groups | What are the relevant geopolitical, regulatory, network, or trust groups? | _Example: Public internet consumers; Edge / DMZ zone; Internal API platform zone; Internal source systems zone_ |
| Group Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those groups, such as residency, trust level, or network exposure? | _Example: Untrusted external traffic; Public entry, filtered access; Trusted internal services; Restricted back-office access_ |
| Relevant Locations / Zones | What are the relevant locations, zones, or environments within each group? | _Example: Browser / mobile app; CDN / API gateway; Search API / cluster; PIM / catalog system; Media storage / CDN_ |
| Location / Zone Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those locations or zones, such as ownership, region, or exposure? | _Example: Public client environment; Internet-facing ingress; Regional read service; System of record; Cached media delivery_ |
| Network / Regulatory Distances | What latency, trust, regulatory, or connectivity distances exist between the locations? | _Example: Client to edge over internet; Edge to API over trusted network; API to search in-region; API to PIM cross-zone_ |
| Distance Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those distances, such as latency sensitivity, residency constraints, or trust boundaries? | _Example: Internet adds variable latency; Cross-zone calls need control; Search path must stay low-latency; Source reads should be minimized_ |
| Connectivity Endpoints | What connectivity endpoints or interfaces are associated with the locations? | _Example: Browser clients; Mobile apps; Partner storefront backends; CDN / API gateway_ |
| Endpoint Access Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those endpoints, such as exposure, protocol, security, or access restrictions? | _Example: Public HTTPS access; Untrusted client traffic; Partner auth for backends; Rate limited at edge_ |

Capacity Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: How much capacity is needed to support expected capability consumption?
Station instruction: Use the Capacity Canvas to capture current and future volumes, peaks, latency, caching, rate limiting, and scaling expectations.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Current Business Volumes | What are the current business volumes and transaction rates? | _Example: ~5k searches/day baseline; ~500 concurrent browse users; ~2-3x detail calls per search_ |
| Future Consumption Trends | What are the anticipated future consumption trends? | _Example: Search growth 12-18% monthly; Mobile traffic dominates queries; Campaign peaks up to 4x baseline_ |
| Peak Load and Availability Requirements | What are the peak load and availability requirements? | _Example: p95 search under 500 ms; Support 4x peak without failure; 99.9% discovery availability_ |
| Caching Strategies | What caching strategies can be used to optimize performance? | _Example: Cache top queries for 5 min; Cache category results for 10 min; Use stale-while-revalidate_ |
| Rate Limiting Strategies | What rate limiting strategies can be used to manage consumption? | _Example: Limit bot-like query bursts; Throttle heavy filter queries; Separate public and partner quotas_ |
| Scaling Strategies | What scaling strategies can be used to accommodate growth? | _Example: Scale read APIs horizontally; Autoscale search nodes on load; Isolate indexing from queries_ |

Integration pattern decision

| Primary pattern | Fallback pattern | Justification |
| API / Event / File exchange / Stream / Data product / Direct integration / Hybrid |  |  |

Reusable capability analysis
Conduct after requirements are defined.

| Candidate | Type | Business fit | Shape match | Timing fit | Gaps / comments |
|  | API / Event / Data product / File / Stream / Existing service |  |  |  |  |
Classification: Reuse as-is / Reuse with interface / Extend / None
Reasoning: 

Pattern option notes

| Candidate pattern | Use when | Fit / concerns | Decision |
| API | Consumers need request-response or query access, clear contracts, interactive workflows, ecosystem onboarding, or productized self-service consumption. |  |  |
| Event | Consumers react to business facts asynchronously, producers should publish state changes once, and loose coupling or fan-out is important. |  |  |
| File exchange | Batch movement, partner constraints, legacy tooling, or reconciliation workflows make file delivery the appropriate contract. |  |  |
| Data product | The primary outcome is trusted analytical or operational data consumption with ownership, quality, lineage, and discoverability expectations. |  |  |
| Stream | High-volume continuous data movement, low-latency processing, or durable ordered event streams are central requirements. |  |  |
| Direct integration | The integration is intentionally local, constrained, low reuse, or temporary, and the exception from reusable capability design is documented. |  |  |

Architecture diagram
Insert a high-level diagram aligned with the target architecture.

Architecture decision record (ADR)
Title: [Describe capability]
Status: Proposed / Approved
Context:
- Key business requirements
- Technical constraints
- Expected volumes / performance
Decision:
- Chosen integration pattern and why
- Reuse, if any, chosen and why
Alternatives considered:
- Rejected approaches and reasons
Consequences:
- Pros, cons, risks, mitigations
Follow-up actions:
- Register APIs, event topics, data products, file contracts, governance checks, or support model updates

| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | _Example: Product: merchandised offering; Variant: purchasable option; Category: browse grouping; SearchQuery: shopper intent; Facet: filterable dimension_ |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | _Example: Product: id, slug, name, status; Variant: sku, options, active; Category: id, name, parent; SearchQuery: term, filters, market; Facet: name, values, counts_ |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | _Example: SearchQuery returns Products; Product has many Variants; Product belongs to Categories; Facet refines SearchQuery; Variant belongs to Product_ |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | _Example: Only published Products appear; Only active Variants selectable; Product ids stay stable; Search ranking should be explainable_ |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | _Example: Catalog data is public read; No PII in SearchQuery; Internal fields stay hidden; Admin writes stay separate_ |

h4. Capability Value Proposition Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
Station instruction: Use the Capability Value Proposition Canvas to capture consumer tasks, gains, pains, and reusable capability features without naming the delivery technology too early.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? |  |

h4. Capability Business Model Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
Station instruction: Use the Capability Business Model Canvas to clarify ownership, partners, channels, costs, benefits, support, and lifecycle expectations for the reusable capability.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? |  |

h3. Consumer Requirements & Onboarding
Station description: Capture consumer onboarding, standards, non-functional requirements, service expectations, constraints, security needs, allowed protocols, data freshness, SLAs, observability, recovery, adoption requirements, and producer responsibilities.
Why it matters: The right architecture depends on consumer goals, onboarding expectations, service levels, data quality needs, change tolerance, observability, support, and producer constraints.

h4. Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
Station instruction: Use the Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas to capture consumer goals, availability, freshness, volume, performance, data quality, security, onboarding, change, observability, and recovery expectations.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? |  |

h3. Architecture & Platform Decisions
Station description: Use requirements and constraints to decide the right architecture pattern and enabling platform capabilities.
Why it matters: Architecture choices should follow from evidence about business impact, locations, trust boundaries, capacity, latency, data ownership, consistency, operability, security, privacy, governance, and cost.

h4. Business Impact Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What are the potential business impacts if the capability, integration, or service fails?
Station instruction: Use the Business Impact Canvas to identify availability, security, and data risks that influence architecture options.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Availability Risks | What are the potential risks to capability availability? | _Example: Search outage blocks discovery; Traffic spikes overload search; Slow responses hurt UX_ |
| Security Risks | What are the potential security risks associated with the capability? | _Example: Public API abused by bots; Internal fields exposed; Query logs leak user intent_ |
| Data Risks | What are the potential risks to data integrity or confidentiality? | _Example: Stale index shows wrong results; Hidden products appear in results; Inconsistent IDs break linking_ |
| Mitigate Availability Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the availability risks? | _Example: Cache popular queries; Autoscale search services; Graceful fallback results_ |
| Mitigate Security Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the security risks? | _Example: Rate limit public access; Filter response fields; Sanitize logs and queries_ |
| Mitigate Data Risks | How can the capability owner mitigate the data risks? | _Example: Validate indexed data; Enforce visibility rules; Monitor index freshness_ |

h4. Locations Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: What geopolitical, regulatory, network, and trust boundaries affect this capability or integration?
Station instruction: Use the Locations Canvas to capture geopolitical, regulatory, network, residency, and trust-boundary constraints.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Location / Trust Groups | What are the relevant geopolitical, regulatory, network, or trust groups? | _Example: Public internet consumers; Edge / DMZ zone; Internal API platform zone; Internal source systems zone_ |
| Group Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those groups, such as residency, trust level, or network exposure? | _Example: Untrusted external traffic; Public entry, filtered access; Trusted internal services; Restricted back-office access_ |
| Relevant Locations / Zones | What are the relevant locations, zones, or environments within each group? | _Example: Browser / mobile app; CDN / API gateway; Search API / cluster; PIM / catalog system; Media storage / CDN_ |
| Location / Zone Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those locations or zones, such as ownership, region, or exposure? | _Example: Public client environment; Internet-facing ingress; Regional read service; System of record; Cached media delivery_ |
| Network / Regulatory Distances | What latency, trust, regulatory, or connectivity distances exist between the locations? | _Example: Client to edge over internet; Edge to API over trusted network; API to search in-region; API to PIM cross-zone_ |
| Distance Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those distances, such as latency sensitivity, residency constraints, or trust boundaries? | _Example: Internet adds variable latency; Cross-zone calls need control; Search path must stay low-latency; Source reads should be minimized_ |
| Connectivity Endpoints | What connectivity endpoints or interfaces are associated with the locations? | _Example: Browser clients; Mobile apps; Partner storefront backends; CDN / API gateway_ |
| Endpoint Access Characteristics | What are the characteristics of those endpoints, such as exposure, protocol, security, or access restrictions? | _Example: Public HTTPS access; Untrusted client traffic; Partner auth for backends; Rate limited at edge_ |

h4. Capacity Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=en
Purpose: How much capacity is needed to support expected capability consumption?
Station instruction: Use the Capacity Canvas to capture current and future volumes, peaks, latency, caching, rate limiting, and scaling expectations.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Current Business Volumes | What are the current business volumes and transaction rates? | _Example: ~5k searches/day baseline; ~500 concurrent browse users; ~2-3x detail calls per search_ |
| Future Consumption Trends | What are the anticipated future consumption trends? | _Example: Search growth 12-18% monthly; Mobile traffic dominates queries; Campaign peaks up to 4x baseline_ |
| Peak Load and Availability Requirements | What are the peak load and availability requirements? | _Example: p95 search under 500 ms; Support 4x peak without failure; 99.9% discovery availability_ |
| Caching Strategies | What caching strategies can be used to optimize performance? | _Example: Cache top queries for 5 min; Cache category results for 10 min; Use stale-while-revalidate_ |
| Rate Limiting Strategies | What rate limiting strategies can be used to manage consumption? | _Example: Limit bot-like query bursts; Throttle heavy filter queries; Separate public and partner quotas_ |
| Scaling Strategies | What scaling strategies can be used to accommodate growth? | _Example: Scale read APIs horizontally; Autoscale search nodes on load; Isolate indexing from queries_ |

h2. Integration pattern decision

|| Primary pattern || Fallback pattern || Justification ||
| API / Event / File exchange / Stream / Data product / Direct integration / Hybrid |  |  |

h2. Reusable capability analysis

Conduct after requirements are defined.

|| Candidate || Type || Business fit || Shape match || Timing fit || Gaps / comments ||
|  | API / Event / Data product / File / Stream / Existing service |  |  |  |  |

Classification: Reuse as-is / Reuse with interface / Extend / None
Reasoning: 

h2. Pattern option notes

|| Candidate pattern || Use when || Fit / concerns || Decision ||
| API | Consumers need request-response or query access, clear contracts, interactive workflows, ecosystem onboarding, or productized self-service consumption. |  |  |
| Event | Consumers react to business facts asynchronously, producers should publish state changes once, and loose coupling or fan-out is important. |  |  |
| File exchange | Batch movement, partner constraints, legacy tooling, or reconciliation workflows make file delivery the appropriate contract. |  |  |
| Data product | The primary outcome is trusted analytical or operational data consumption with ownership, quality, lineage, and discoverability expectations. |  |  |
| Stream | High-volume continuous data movement, low-latency processing, or durable ordered event streams are central requirements. |  |  |
| Direct integration | The integration is intentionally local, constrained, low reuse, or temporary, and the exception from reusable capability design is documented. |  |  |

h2. Architecture diagram

Insert a high-level diagram aligned with the target architecture.

h2. Architecture decision record (ADR)

Title: [Describe capability]
Status: Proposed / Approved

Context:
* Key business requirements
* Technical constraints
* Expected volumes / performance

Decision:
* Chosen integration pattern and why
* Reuse, if any, chosen and why

Alternatives considered:
* Rejected approaches and reasons

Consequences:
* Pros, cons, risks, mitigations

Follow-up actions:
* Register APIs, event topics, data products, file contracts, governance checks, or support model updates

en.body:
# Automation Cycle

A cycle for identifying, designing, delivering, enabling, and improving automation opportunities.

Purpose: Productize automation opportunities into governed workflows with clear users, platforms, controls, delivery paths, enablement, and monitoring.
Audience: automation-owner, process-owner, automation-engineer, platform-owner

## Route

Automation Opportunity Strategy -> Process/User Requirements -> Automation Platform Decision -> Automation Workflow Design -> Automation Delivery -> Automation Readiness Review -> Automation Enablement -> Automation Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Automation Opportunity Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: What customer, partner, or consumer journey is the capability intended to support?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Customer Discovers Need | How does the customer recognize their need or problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Who is the typical customer experiencing this journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pains | What are the customer's pain points or challenges? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Journey Steps | What are the steps the customer takes in their journey? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Need Is Resolved | How is the customer's need ultimately resolved? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gains | What are the customer's gains or benefits? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | What are the inputs and outputs at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaction & Processing Rules | What are the interaction and processing rules at each step? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Automation Opportunity Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: What are the core entities and business rules related to this capability or domain?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Selected Customer Journey Steps | Which customer journey steps are relevant to this domain? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Core Entities & Business Meaning | What are the core entities and their business meaning? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attributes & Business Importance | What are the key attributes of each entity and their business importance? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relationships Between Entities | What are the relationships between the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Business, Compliance & Integrity Rules | What are the business, compliance, and integrity rules related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security & Privacy Considerations | What are the security and privacy considerations related to the entities? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Process/User Requirements - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Automation Workflow Design - Interaction Canvas

Purpose: What kinds of interactions should this capability support before choosing a protocol-specific design?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=interactionCanvas&locale=en

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CRUD Interactions | Are CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) interactions needed here? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| CRUD Input & Output Models | What are the input and output models for the CRUD interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| CRUD Processing & Validation | What are the processing and validation rules for the CRUD interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Query-Driven Interactions | What read or query interactions are needed to answer consumer questions? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Query-Driven Input & Output Models | What are the input and output models for the query-driven interactions? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Query-Driven Processing & Validation | What are the processing and validation rules for the query-driven interactions? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Command-Driven Interactions | What state-changing commands are needed, if any? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Command-Driven Input & Output Models | What are the input and output models for the command-driven interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Command-Driven Processing & Validation | What are the processing and validation rules for the command-driven interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Event-Driven Interactions | What events need to be published or consumed, if any? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Event-Driven Input & Output Models | What are the input and output models for the event-driven interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Event-Driven Processing & Validation | What are the processing and validation rules for the event-driven interactions, if this style is needed? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
