de.body:
# Integration Productization Cycle

A cycle for productizing reusable integration capabilities before selecting a concrete integration pattern.

Purpose: Guide reusable integration capability design before choosing APIs, events, files, streams, data products, direct integration, or hybrid patterns.
Audience: platform-architect, integration-architect, platform-owner

## Route

Integration Capability Strategy -> Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Integration Architecture Decision -> Integration Solution Design -> Integration Delivery & Operations -> Integration Readiness Assurance -> Capability Publishing -> Integration Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Integration Capability Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: Welche Reise eines Kunden oder Partners soll mit der API unterstützt werden?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kunde erkennt Bedarf | Wie erkennt der Kunde oder Nutzer ein Bedürfnis oder Problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Wer ist der typische Kunde oder Nutzer, der diese Reise erlebt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Probleme | Was sind die Probleme oder Herausforderungen des Kunden? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Schritte durchläuft der Kunde bei der Reise? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Kundenbedürfnis wird gelöst | Wie wird das Bedürfnis des Kunden letztendlich gelöst? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Nutzen | Welchen Gewinn oder Nutzen hat der Kunde? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | Was sind die Inputs und Outputs bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln | Wie lauten die Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: Was sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und Geschäftsregeln im Zusammenhang mit der API?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ausgewählte Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Customer Journey Schritte sind für diese Domäne relevant? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Grundlegende Entitäten & geschäftliche Bedeutung | Welches sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und ihre Bedeutung für das Geschäft? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attribute & geschäftliche Wichtigkeit | Welches sind die Schlüsselattribute der einzelnen Entitäten und ihre geschäftliche Wichtigkeit? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Beziehungen zwischen Entitäten | Welche Beziehungen bestehen zwischen den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln | Welche Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln gelten für die Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Überlegungen zu Sicherheit und Datenschutz | Welche Überlegungen zur Sicherheit und zum Datenschutz gibt es im Zusammenhang mit den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

de.body:
h1. Integration Productization Cycle

A cycle for productizing reusable integration capabilities before selecting a concrete integration pattern.

Purpose: Guide reusable integration capability design before choosing APIs, events, files, streams, data products, direct integration, or hybrid patterns.
Audience: platform-architect, integration-architect, platform-owner

h2. Route

Integration Capability Strategy -> Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Integration Architecture Decision -> Integration Solution Design -> Integration Delivery & Operations -> Integration Readiness Assurance -> Capability Publishing -> Integration Monitoring & Improvement

h2. Intent & Fit

Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

h2. Requirements

Gather expert input using the recommended questions. Treat answers as inputs to design decisions, not final design decisions.

h2. Integration Pattern Decision

Compare APIs, events, files, streams, data products, direct integration, and hybrid options against requirements.

h2. Architecture Decision Record

Record the selected decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences, evidence, and follow-up actions.

h2. Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

h3. Integration Capability Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: Welche Reise eines Kunden oder Partners soll mit der API unterstützt werden?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=de

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Kunde erkennt Bedarf | Wie erkennt der Kunde oder Nutzer ein Bedürfnis oder Problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

| Probleme | Was sind die Probleme oder Herausforderungen des Kunden? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Schritte durchläuft der Kunde bei der Reise? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Kundenbedürfnis wird gelöst | Wie wird das Bedürfnis des Kunden letztendlich gelöst? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Nutzen | Welchen Gewinn oder Nutzen hat der Kunde? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | Was sind die Inputs und Outputs bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln | Wie lauten die Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Capability Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: Was sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und Geschäftsregeln im Zusammenhang mit der API?

| Attribute & geschäftliche Wichtigkeit | Welches sind die Schlüsselattribute der einzelnen Entitäten und ihre geschäftliche Wichtigkeit? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Beziehungen zwischen Entitäten | Welche Beziehungen bestehen zwischen den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln | Welche Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln gelten für die Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Überlegungen zu Sicherheit und Datenschutz | Welche Überlegungen zur Sicherheit und zum Datenschutz gibt es im Zusammenhang mit den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=de

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Capability Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=de

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||

| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

h3. Integration Consumer Requirements & Onboarding - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?

| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

sections.title: Intent & Fit
sections.description: Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

de.body:
Use this page to gather technology-agnostic integration requirements before deciding whether the solution should be an API, event, file exchange, stream, data product, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.

Intent & fit needs
Purpose: Quickly capture the integration's job-to-be-done and key context.

| Field | Value |
| Integration / capability name |  |
| Job to be done | What business outcome must be achieved? |
| Producer / source systems |  |
| Consumer / target systems |  |
| Core business nouns | Order, Product, Customer... |
| Trigger / event | What initiates the process? |
| Non-functional drivers | Latency, availability, privacy, regulatory, volume, recovery |
| Scope / out of scope |  |
| Decision status | Requirements gathering |

Method path

Line: Core Capability Method
Path: API-Produktstrategie - APIs in strategische Produkte verwandeln -> API Consumer Experience - Design für die echten Benutzer Ihrer API -> API-Plattformarchitektur - Architektur von APIs für Skalierbarkeit und Wiederverwendung -> API-Design - Entwerfen Sie APIs, die Wert liefern -> API-Bereitstellung - Bereitstellung sicherer und zuverlässiger APIs -> API-Audit - Prüfung von APIs auf Konformität und Qualität -> API-Veröffentlichung - Veröffentlichen Sie APIs mit Zuversicht -> Überwachen und Verbessern - für den API-Wert

Text sources
Integration document canvas order and station grouping: src/data/method/integration-extension.json
Integration line and station descriptions: src/data/method/lines.json, src/data/method/stations.json, and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.*.json
Canvas title, purpose, and questions: src/data/canvas/localizedData.json
Station descriptions and instructions: src/data/method/stations.json and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.stations.json
Example answers, when available: src/data/canvas/import-export-templates/*.json

Business flow visualization
Add a simple process outline or Mermaid diagram showing producer, consumer, trigger, data movement, and key decision points.

Requirements

API-Produktstrategie - APIs in strategische Produkte verwandeln
Station description: Bevor Sie etwas bauen, sollten Sie von Anfang an den Wert, die Benutzer und die Geschäftsziele Ihrer API definieren.
Why it matters: Viele Unternehmen betrachten APIs als technische Projekte und nicht als Produkte. Das Ergebnis? Verwirrte Kunden, schlechte Akzeptanz und verschwendeter Aufwand.  Diese Station hilft Ihnen, den Zweck Ihrer API, die Zielgruppe und die Erfolgskriterien zu definieren, damit Teams APIs bereitstellen können, die echte Probleme lösen.

Customer Journey Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche Reise eines Kunden oder Partners soll mit der API unterstützt werden?
Station instruction: Untersuchen Sie das Kunden- oder Partnerproblem, das Sie mit APIs lösen wollen. Zeichnen Sie die Stakeholder, deren Wege und Ergebnisse mithilfe der Customer Journey Canvas auf.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Kunde erkennt Bedarf | Wie erkennt der Kunde oder Nutzer ein Bedürfnis oder Problem? | _Example: Need to find the right product_ |
| Persona | Wer ist der typische Kunde oder Nutzer, der diese Reise erlebt? | _Example: Mobile shopper comparing options; Wants quick, confident choice_ |
| Probleme | Was sind die Probleme oder Herausforderungen des Kunden? | _Example: Too many irrelevant results; Filters are hard to understand; Price or stock looks stale_ |
| Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Schritte durchläuft der Kunde bei der Reise? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Kundenbedürfnis wird gelöst | Wie wird das Bedürfnis des Kunden letztendlich gelöst? | _Example: Find the right product fast_ |
| Nutzen | Welchen Gewinn oder Nutzen hat der Kunde? | _Example: Relevant results quickly; Easy comparison on mobile; Confident variant choice_ |
| Inputs & Outputs | Was sind die Inputs und Outputs bei jedem Schritt? | _Example: Inputs: need, keyword, filters; Outputs: options, detail, choice_ |
| Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln | Wie lauten die Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln bei jedem Schritt? | _Example: Show relevant results first; Keep filters simple; Hide unavailable options_ |

Domain Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Was sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und Geschäftsregeln im Zusammenhang mit der API?
Station instruction: Definieren Sie zentrale Entitäten, ihre Attribute und Beziehungen, um ein gemeinsames konzeptionelles Verständnis für alle Journeys und eventuell APIs zu schaffen.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Ausgewählte Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Customer Journey Schritte sind für diese Domäne relevant? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Grundlegende Entitäten & geschäftliche Bedeutung | Welches sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und ihre Bedeutung für das Geschäft? | _Example: Product: merchandised offering; Variant: purchasable option; Category: browse grouping; SearchQuery: shopper intent; Facet: filterable dimension_ |
| Attribute & geschäftliche Wichtigkeit | Welches sind die Schlüsselattribute der einzelnen Entitäten und ihre geschäftliche Wichtigkeit? | _Example: Product: id, slug, name, status; Variant: sku, options, active; Category: id, name, parent; SearchQuery: term, filters, market; Facet: name, values, counts_ |
| Beziehungen zwischen Entitäten | Welche Beziehungen bestehen zwischen den Entitäten? | _Example: SearchQuery returns Products; Product has many Variants; Product belongs to Categories; Facet refines SearchQuery; Variant belongs to Product_ |
| Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln | Welche Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln gelten für die Entitäten? | _Example: Only published Products appear; Only active Variants selectable; Product ids stay stable; Search ranking should be explainable_ |
| Überlegungen zu Sicherheit und Datenschutz | Welche Überlegungen zur Sicherheit und zum Datenschutz gibt es im Zusammenhang mit den Entitäten? | _Example: Catalog data is public read; No PII in SearchQuery; Internal fields stay hidden; Admin writes stay separate_ |

Capability Value Proposition Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
Station instruction: Verwenden Sie das API Value Proposition Canvas, um die unterstützten Geschäftsaufgaben sowie die Probleme, Vorteile und Funktionen für API-Nutzer zu erfassen, die neue oder wiederverwendbare APIs prägen.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? |  |

Capability Business Model Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
Station instruction: Definieren Sie den Wert - für die Nutzer und das Unternehmen - mit dem API Business Model Canvas, um Ihre API-Strategie visuell, gemeinsam nutzbar und einfach mit Ihren API-Kunden an der nächsten Station zu validieren.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? |  |

API Consumer Experience - Design für die echten Benutzer Ihrer API
Station description: Stellen Sie sicher, dass Ihre API auffindbar, verständlich und nutzbar ist - vor und nach der Einführung.
Why it matters: Großartige APIs funktionieren nicht nur - sie fühlen sich auch intuitiv an. Ganz gleich, ob Ihr Kunde ein interner Entwickler, ein externer Partner oder ein KI-Agent ist, seine Erfahrung entscheidet über die Akzeptanz.  Ohne einen klaren Erlebnisplan: - Großartige APIs bleiben ungenutzt - Teams verschwenden Zeit damit, zu raten, wie sie Ihre API verwenden sollen - Feedbackschleifen sind unterbrochen oder fehlen.  Diese Station hilft Ihnen, Ihre API mit den Augen ihrer Nutzer zu sehen.

Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
Station instruction: Überprüfen Sie das bestehende API Value Proposition Canvаs von der Perspektive des API-Nutzers. Stellen Sie die Stabilität der unterstützten Geschäftsprozesse sicher und ergänzen Sie die Funktionen, die Probleme lösen und Vorteile bieten, um die Bedürfnisse der API-Nutzer zu berücksichtigen, damit diese spätere Designentscheidungen leiten können.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? |  |

API-Plattformarchitektur - Architektur von APIs für Skalierbarkeit und Wiederverwendung
Station description: Gewährleisten Sie Skalierbarkeit, Wiederverwendung und Governance für Ihre API- und Plattformkomponenten.
Why it matters: Wenn APIs teamübergreifend skaliert werden, muss Ihre Plattform Governance und Wiederverwendung ermöglichen, ohne die Geschwindigkeit zu beeinträchtigen. Diese Station zeigt, wie man APIs für Langlebigkeit, Sicherheit und Effizienz entwickelt.

Business Impact Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Was sind die potenziellen geschäftlichen Auswirkungen eines Ausfalls einer API?
Station instruction: Das Business Impact Canvas hilft dabei, Risiken in Bezug auf API-Verfügbarkeit, -Sicherheit und -Funktionalität zu identifizieren und zu mindern, um fundierte Architekturentscheidungen zu unterstützen.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Risiken der Verfügbarkeit | Was sind die potenziellen Risiken für die API-Verfügbarkeit? | _Example: Search outage blocks discovery; Traffic spikes overload search; Slow responses hurt UX_ |
| Sicherheitsrisiken | Welche potenziellen Sicherheitsrisiken sind mit der API assoziiert? | _Example: Public API abused by bots; Internal fields exposed; Query logs leak user intent_ |
| Datenrisiken | Welche potenziellen Risiken bestehen für die Datenintegrität oder -Vertraulichkeit? | _Example: Stale index shows wrong results; Hidden products appear in results; Inconsistent IDs break linking_ |
| Reduzierung von Risiken der Verfügbarkeit | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Risiken der Verfügbarkeit reduzieren? | _Example: Cache popular queries; Autoscale search services; Graceful fallback results_ |
| Reduktion der Sicherheitsrisiken | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Sicherheitsrisiken reduzieren? | _Example: Rate limit public access; Filter response fields; Sanitize logs and queries_ |
| Reduktion der Datenrisiken | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Datenrisiken reduzieren? | _Example: Validate indexed data; Enforce visibility rules; Monitor index freshness_ |

Location Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche geopolitischen, regulatorischen, netzwerkbezogenen und vertrauensbezogenen Grenzen wirken sich auf diese API aus?
Station instruction: Kartierung der Standorte von API-Anbietern und -Verbrauchern, um die Zugänglichkeit und Einhaltung von Vorschriften zu gewährleisten.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Standort-/Vertrauensgruppen | Welche geopolitischen, regulatorischen, netzwerkbezogenen oder vertrauensbezogenen Gruppen sind relevant? | _Example: Public internet consumers; Edge / DMZ zone; Internal API platform zone; Internal source systems zone_ |
| Gruppenmerkmale | Was sind die Merkmale dieser Gruppen, wie z. B. Standort, Vertrauensstufe oder Netzwerkexposition? | _Example: Untrusted external traffic; Public entry, filtered access; Trusted internal services; Restricted back-office access_ |
| Relevante Standorte / Zonen | Was sind die relevanten Standorte, Zonen oder Umgebungen innerhalb jeder Gruppe? | _Example: Browser / mobile app; CDN / API gateway; Search API / cluster; PIM / catalog system; Media storage / CDN_ |
| Standort-/Zonenmerkmale | Was sind die Merkmale dieser Standorte oder Zonen, wie z. B. Eigentumsverhältnisse, Region oder Exposition? | _Example: Public client environment; Internet-facing ingress; Regional read service; System of record; Cached media delivery_ |
| Netzwerk- / Regulierungsentfernungen | Welche Latenz-, Vertrauens-, Regulierungs- oder Konnektivitätsentfernungen bestehen zwischen den Standorten? | _Example: Client to edge over internet; Edge to API over trusted network; API to search in-region; API to PIM cross-zone_ |
| Entfernungsmerkmale | Was sind die Merkmale dieser Entfernungen, wie z. B. Latenzempfindlichkeit, Aufenthaltsbeschränkungen oder Vertrauensgrenzen? | _Example: Internet adds variable latency; Cross-zone calls need control; Search path must stay low-latency; Source reads should be minimized_ |
| Konnektivitätsendpunkte | Welche Konnektivitätsendpunkte oder Schnittstellen sind mit den Standorten verbunden? | _Example: Browser clients; Mobile apps; Partner storefront backends; CDN / API gateway_ |
| Endpunktzugriffseigenschaften | Welche Eigenschaften weisen diese Endpunkte auf, wie z. B. Sichtbarkeit, Protokoll, Sicherheit oder Zugriffsbeschränkungen? | _Example: Public HTTPS access; Untrusted client traffic; Partner auth for backends; Rate limited at edge_ |

Capacity Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche Anforderungen ergeben sich, um den Konsum der API zu gewährleisten?
Station instruction: Das Capacity Canvas gleicht geschäftliche Transaktionsmuster, künftige Verbrauchstrends und technische Lösungen ab, um die Skalierbarkeit und Leistung von APIs zu gewährleisten und liefert wichtige Informationen für Skalierungsentscheidungen und die Infrastrukturplanung.

| Topic | Question | Answer / evidence / owner |
| Aktuelles Geschäftsvolumen | Wie hoch sind das aktuelle Geschäftsvolumen und die Transaktionsraten? | _Example: ~5k searches/day baseline; ~500 concurrent browse users; ~2-3x detail calls per search_ |
| Zukünftige Konsumtrends | Was sind die erwarteten künftigen Konsumtrends? | _Example: Search growth 12-18% monthly; Mobile traffic dominates queries; Campaign peaks up to 4x baseline_ |
| Spitzenlast- und Verfügbarkeitsanforderungen | Was sind die Spitzenlast- und Verfügbarkeitsanforderungen? | _Example: p95 search under 500 ms; Support 4x peak without failure; 99.9% discovery availability_ |
| Caching-Strategien | Welche Caching-Strategien können zur Performanceoptimierung eingesetzt werden? | _Example: Cache top queries for 5 min; Cache category results for 10 min; Use stale-while-revalidate_ |
| Rate-Limiting-Strategien | Welche Rate-Limiting-Strategien können zur Konsumsteuerung eingesetzt werden? | _Example: Limit bot-like query bursts; Throttle heavy filter queries; Separate public and partner quotas_ |
| Strategien zur Skalierung | Welche Strategien zur Skalierung können eingesetzt werden, um das Wachstum zu unterstützen? | _Example: Scale read APIs horizontally; Autoscale search nodes on load; Isolate indexing from queries_ |

Integration pattern decision

| Primary pattern | Fallback pattern | Justification |
| API / Event / File exchange / Stream / Data product / Direct integration / Hybrid |  |  |

Reusable capability analysis
Conduct after requirements are defined.

| Candidate | Type | Business fit | Shape match | Timing fit | Gaps / comments |
|  | API / Event / Data product / File / Stream / Existing service |  |  |  |  |
Classification: Reuse as-is / Reuse with interface / Extend / None
Reasoning: 

Pattern option notes

| Candidate pattern | Use when | Fit / concerns | Decision |
| API | Consumers need request-response or query access, clear contracts, interactive workflows, ecosystem onboarding, or productized self-service consumption. |  |  |
| Event | Consumers react to business facts asynchronously, producers should publish state changes once, and loose coupling or fan-out is important. |  |  |
| File exchange | Batch movement, partner constraints, legacy tooling, or reconciliation workflows make file delivery the appropriate contract. |  |  |
| Data product | The primary outcome is trusted analytical or operational data consumption with ownership, quality, lineage, and discoverability expectations. |  |  |
| Stream | High-volume continuous data movement, low-latency processing, or durable ordered event streams are central requirements. |  |  |
| Direct integration | The integration is intentionally local, constrained, low reuse, or temporary, and the exception from reusable capability design is documented. |  |  |

Architecture diagram
Insert a high-level diagram aligned with the target architecture.

Architecture decision record (ADR)
Title: [Describe capability]
Status: Proposed / Approved
Context:
- Key business requirements
- Technical constraints
- Expected volumes / performance
Decision:
- Chosen integration pattern and why
- Reuse, if any, chosen and why
Alternatives considered:
- Rejected approaches and reasons
Consequences:
- Pros, cons, risks, mitigations
Follow-up actions:
- Register APIs, event topics, data products, file contracts, governance checks, or support model updates

de.title: Integration Productization Cycle integration design Confluence wiki

sections.title: Intent & Fit
sections.description: Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

sections.title: Requirements
sections.description: Gather expert input using the recommended questions. Treat answers as inputs to design decisions, not final design decisions.

sections.title: Integration Pattern Decision
sections.description: Compare APIs, events, files, streams, data products, direct integration, and hybrid options against requirements.

sections.title: Architecture Decision Record
sections.description: Record the selected decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences, evidence, and follow-up actions.

de.body:
Use this page to gather technology-agnostic integration requirements before deciding whether the solution should be an API, event, file exchange, stream, data product, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.

h2. Intent & fit needs

Purpose: Quickly capture the integration's job-to-be-done and key context.

|| Field || Value ||
| Integration / capability name |  |
| Job to be done | What business outcome must be achieved? |
| Producer / source systems |  |
| Consumer / target systems |  |
| Core business nouns | Order, Product, Customer... |
| Trigger / event | What initiates the process? |
| Non-functional drivers | Latency, availability, privacy, regulatory, volume, recovery |
| Scope / out of scope |  |
| Decision status | Requirements gathering |

h2. Method path

Line: Core Capability Method
Path: API-Produktstrategie - APIs in strategische Produkte verwandeln -> API Consumer Experience - Design für die echten Benutzer Ihrer API -> API-Plattformarchitektur - Architektur von APIs für Skalierbarkeit und Wiederverwendung -> API-Design - Entwerfen Sie APIs, die Wert liefern -> API-Bereitstellung - Bereitstellung sicherer und zuverlässiger APIs -> API-Audit - Prüfung von APIs auf Konformität und Qualität -> API-Veröffentlichung - Veröffentlichen Sie APIs mit Zuversicht -> Überwachen und Verbessern - für den API-Wert

h2. Text sources

* Integration document canvas order and station grouping: src/data/method/integration-extension.json
* Integration line and station descriptions: src/data/method/lines.json, src/data/method/stations.json, and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.*.json
* Canvas title, purpose, and questions: src/data/canvas/localizedData.json
* Station descriptions and instructions: src/data/method/stations.json and src/data/method/<locale>/labels.stations.json
* Example answers, when available: src/data/canvas/import-export-templates/*.json

h2. Business flow visualization

Add a simple process outline or Mermaid diagram showing producer, consumer, trigger, data movement, and key decision points.

h2. Requirements

h3. API-Produktstrategie - APIs in strategische Produkte verwandeln
Station description: Bevor Sie etwas bauen, sollten Sie von Anfang an den Wert, die Benutzer und die Geschäftsziele Ihrer API definieren.
Why it matters: Viele Unternehmen betrachten APIs als technische Projekte und nicht als Produkte. Das Ergebnis? Verwirrte Kunden, schlechte Akzeptanz und verschwendeter Aufwand.  Diese Station hilft Ihnen, den Zweck Ihrer API, die Zielgruppe und die Erfolgskriterien zu definieren, damit Teams APIs bereitstellen können, die echte Probleme lösen.

h4. Customer Journey Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche Reise eines Kunden oder Partners soll mit der API unterstützt werden?
Station instruction: Untersuchen Sie das Kunden- oder Partnerproblem, das Sie mit APIs lösen wollen. Zeichnen Sie die Stakeholder, deren Wege und Ergebnisse mithilfe der Customer Journey Canvas auf.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Kunde erkennt Bedarf | Wie erkennt der Kunde oder Nutzer ein Bedürfnis oder Problem? | _Example: Need to find the right product_ |
| Persona | Wer ist der typische Kunde oder Nutzer, der diese Reise erlebt? | _Example: Mobile shopper comparing options; Wants quick, confident choice_ |
| Probleme | Was sind die Probleme oder Herausforderungen des Kunden? | _Example: Too many irrelevant results; Filters are hard to understand; Price or stock looks stale_ |
| Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Schritte durchläuft der Kunde bei der Reise? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Kundenbedürfnis wird gelöst | Wie wird das Bedürfnis des Kunden letztendlich gelöst? | _Example: Find the right product fast_ |
| Nutzen | Welchen Gewinn oder Nutzen hat der Kunde? | _Example: Relevant results quickly; Easy comparison on mobile; Confident variant choice_ |
| Inputs & Outputs | Was sind die Inputs und Outputs bei jedem Schritt? | _Example: Inputs: need, keyword, filters; Outputs: options, detail, choice_ |
| Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln | Wie lauten die Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln bei jedem Schritt? | _Example: Show relevant results first; Keep filters simple; Hide unavailable options_ |

h4. Domain Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Was sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und Geschäftsregeln im Zusammenhang mit der API?
Station instruction: Definieren Sie zentrale Entitäten, ihre Attribute und Beziehungen, um ein gemeinsames konzeptionelles Verständnis für alle Journeys und eventuell APIs zu schaffen.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Ausgewählte Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Customer Journey Schritte sind für diese Domäne relevant? | _Example: Search catalog; Filter results; Compare options; Open product; Add variant to cart_ |
| Grundlegende Entitäten & geschäftliche Bedeutung | Welches sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und ihre Bedeutung für das Geschäft? | _Example: Product: merchandised offering; Variant: purchasable option; Category: browse grouping; SearchQuery: shopper intent; Facet: filterable dimension_ |
| Attribute & geschäftliche Wichtigkeit | Welches sind die Schlüsselattribute der einzelnen Entitäten und ihre geschäftliche Wichtigkeit? | _Example: Product: id, slug, name, status; Variant: sku, options, active; Category: id, name, parent; SearchQuery: term, filters, market; Facet: name, values, counts_ |
| Beziehungen zwischen Entitäten | Welche Beziehungen bestehen zwischen den Entitäten? | _Example: SearchQuery returns Products; Product has many Variants; Product belongs to Categories; Facet refines SearchQuery; Variant belongs to Product_ |
| Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln | Welche Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln gelten für die Entitäten? | _Example: Only published Products appear; Only active Variants selectable; Product ids stay stable; Search ranking should be explainable_ |
| Überlegungen zu Sicherheit und Datenschutz | Welche Überlegungen zur Sicherheit und zum Datenschutz gibt es im Zusammenhang mit den Entitäten? | _Example: Catalog data is public read; No PII in SearchQuery; Internal fields stay hidden; Admin writes stay separate_ |

h4. Capability Value Proposition Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
Station instruction: Verwenden Sie das API Value Proposition Canvas, um die unterstützten Geschäftsaufgaben sowie die Probleme, Vorteile und Funktionen für API-Nutzer zu erfassen, die neue oder wiederverwendbare APIs prägen.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? |  |

h4. Capability Business Model Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
Station instruction: Definieren Sie den Wert - für die Nutzer und das Unternehmen - mit dem API Business Model Canvas, um Ihre API-Strategie visuell, gemeinsam nutzbar und einfach mit Ihren API-Kunden an der nächsten Station zu validieren.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? |  |

h3. API Consumer Experience - Design für die echten Benutzer Ihrer API
Station description: Stellen Sie sicher, dass Ihre API auffindbar, verständlich und nutzbar ist - vor und nach der Einführung.
Why it matters: Großartige APIs funktionieren nicht nur - sie fühlen sich auch intuitiv an. Ganz gleich, ob Ihr Kunde ein interner Entwickler, ein externer Partner oder ein KI-Agent ist, seine Erfahrung entscheidet über die Akzeptanz.  Ohne einen klaren Erlebnisplan: - Großartige APIs bleiben ungenutzt - Teams verschwenden Zeit damit, zu raten, wie sie Ihre API verwenden sollen - Feedbackschleifen sind unterbrochen oder fehlen.  Diese Station hilft Ihnen, Ihre API mit den Augen ihrer Nutzer zu sehen.

h4. Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
Station instruction: Überprüfen Sie das bestehende API Value Proposition Canvаs von der Perspektive des API-Nutzers. Stellen Sie die Stabilität der unterstützten Geschäftsprozesse sicher und ergänzen Sie die Funktionen, die Probleme lösen und Vorteile bieten, um die Bedürfnisse der API-Nutzer zu berücksichtigen, damit diese spätere Designentscheidungen leiten können.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? |  |

h3. API-Plattformarchitektur - Architektur von APIs für Skalierbarkeit und Wiederverwendung
Station description: Gewährleisten Sie Skalierbarkeit, Wiederverwendung und Governance für Ihre API- und Plattformkomponenten.
Why it matters: Wenn APIs teamübergreifend skaliert werden, muss Ihre Plattform Governance und Wiederverwendung ermöglichen, ohne die Geschwindigkeit zu beeinträchtigen. Diese Station zeigt, wie man APIs für Langlebigkeit, Sicherheit und Effizienz entwickelt.

h4. Business Impact Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Was sind die potenziellen geschäftlichen Auswirkungen eines Ausfalls einer API?
Station instruction: Das Business Impact Canvas hilft dabei, Risiken in Bezug auf API-Verfügbarkeit, -Sicherheit und -Funktionalität zu identifizieren und zu mindern, um fundierte Architekturentscheidungen zu unterstützen.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Risiken der Verfügbarkeit | Was sind die potenziellen Risiken für die API-Verfügbarkeit? | _Example: Search outage blocks discovery; Traffic spikes overload search; Slow responses hurt UX_ |
| Sicherheitsrisiken | Welche potenziellen Sicherheitsrisiken sind mit der API assoziiert? | _Example: Public API abused by bots; Internal fields exposed; Query logs leak user intent_ |
| Datenrisiken | Welche potenziellen Risiken bestehen für die Datenintegrität oder -Vertraulichkeit? | _Example: Stale index shows wrong results; Hidden products appear in results; Inconsistent IDs break linking_ |
| Reduzierung von Risiken der Verfügbarkeit | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Risiken der Verfügbarkeit reduzieren? | _Example: Cache popular queries; Autoscale search services; Graceful fallback results_ |
| Reduktion der Sicherheitsrisiken | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Sicherheitsrisiken reduzieren? | _Example: Rate limit public access; Filter response fields; Sanitize logs and queries_ |
| Reduktion der Datenrisiken | Wie kann der API-Anbieter die Datenrisiken reduzieren? | _Example: Validate indexed data; Enforce visibility rules; Monitor index freshness_ |

h4. Location Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=locationsCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche geopolitischen, regulatorischen, netzwerkbezogenen und vertrauensbezogenen Grenzen wirken sich auf diese API aus?
Station instruction: Kartierung der Standorte von API-Anbietern und -Verbrauchern, um die Zugänglichkeit und Einhaltung von Vorschriften zu gewährleisten.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Standort-/Vertrauensgruppen | Welche geopolitischen, regulatorischen, netzwerkbezogenen oder vertrauensbezogenen Gruppen sind relevant? | _Example: Public internet consumers; Edge / DMZ zone; Internal API platform zone; Internal source systems zone_ |

| Standort-/Zonenmerkmale | Was sind die Merkmale dieser Standorte oder Zonen, wie z. B. Eigentumsverhältnisse, Region oder Exposition? | _Example: Public client environment; Internet-facing ingress; Regional read service; System of record; Cached media delivery_ |
| Netzwerk- / Regulierungsentfernungen | Welche Latenz-, Vertrauens-, Regulierungs- oder Konnektivitätsentfernungen bestehen zwischen den Standorten? | _Example: Client to edge over internet; Edge to API over trusted network; API to search in-region; API to PIM cross-zone_ |
| Entfernungsmerkmale | Was sind die Merkmale dieser Entfernungen, wie z. B. Latenzempfindlichkeit, Aufenthaltsbeschränkungen oder Vertrauensgrenzen? | _Example: Internet adds variable latency; Cross-zone calls need control; Search path must stay low-latency; Source reads should be minimized_ |
| Konnektivitätsendpunkte | Welche Konnektivitätsendpunkte oder Schnittstellen sind mit den Standorten verbunden? | _Example: Browser clients; Mobile apps; Partner storefront backends; CDN / API gateway_ |
| Endpunktzugriffseigenschaften | Welche Eigenschaften weisen diese Endpunkte auf, wie z. B. Sichtbarkeit, Protokoll, Sicherheit oder Zugriffsbeschränkungen? | _Example: Public HTTPS access; Untrusted client traffic; Partner auth for backends; Rate limited at edge_ |

h4. Capacity Canvas
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capacityCanvas&locale=de
Purpose: Welche Anforderungen ergeben sich, um den Konsum der API zu gewährleisten?
Station instruction: Das Capacity Canvas gleicht geschäftliche Transaktionsmuster, künftige Verbrauchstrends und technische Lösungen ab, um die Skalierbarkeit und Leistung von APIs zu gewährleisten und liefert wichtige Informationen für Skalierungsentscheidungen und die Infrastrukturplanung.

|| Topic || Question || Answer / evidence / owner ||
| Aktuelles Geschäftsvolumen | Wie hoch sind das aktuelle Geschäftsvolumen und die Transaktionsraten? | _Example: ~5k searches/day baseline; ~500 concurrent browse users; ~2-3x detail calls per search_ |
| Zukünftige Konsumtrends | Was sind die erwarteten künftigen Konsumtrends? | _Example: Search growth 12-18% monthly; Mobile traffic dominates queries; Campaign peaks up to 4x baseline_ |
| Spitzenlast- und Verfügbarkeitsanforderungen | Was sind die Spitzenlast- und Verfügbarkeitsanforderungen? | _Example: p95 search under 500 ms; Support 4x peak without failure; 99.9% discovery availability_ |
| Caching-Strategien | Welche Caching-Strategien können zur Performanceoptimierung eingesetzt werden? | _Example: Cache top queries for 5 min; Cache category results for 10 min; Use stale-while-revalidate_ |
| Rate-Limiting-Strategien | Welche Rate-Limiting-Strategien können zur Konsumsteuerung eingesetzt werden? | _Example: Limit bot-like query bursts; Throttle heavy filter queries; Separate public and partner quotas_ |
| Strategien zur Skalierung | Welche Strategien zur Skalierung können eingesetzt werden, um das Wachstum zu unterstützen? | _Example: Scale read APIs horizontally; Autoscale search nodes on load; Isolate indexing from queries_ |

h2. Integration pattern decision

|| Primary pattern || Fallback pattern || Justification ||
| API / Event / File exchange / Stream / Data product / Direct integration / Hybrid |  |  |

h2. Reusable capability analysis

Conduct after requirements are defined.

|| Candidate || Type || Business fit || Shape match || Timing fit || Gaps / comments ||
|  | API / Event / Data product / File / Stream / Existing service |  |  |  |  |

Classification: Reuse as-is / Reuse with interface / Extend / None
Reasoning: 

h2. Pattern option notes

|| Candidate pattern || Use when || Fit / concerns || Decision ||
| API | Consumers need request-response or query access, clear contracts, interactive workflows, ecosystem onboarding, or productized self-service consumption. |  |  |
| Event | Consumers react to business facts asynchronously, producers should publish state changes once, and loose coupling or fan-out is important. |  |  |
| File exchange | Batch movement, partner constraints, legacy tooling, or reconciliation workflows make file delivery the appropriate contract. |  |  |
| Data product | The primary outcome is trusted analytical or operational data consumption with ownership, quality, lineage, and discoverability expectations. |  |  |
| Stream | High-volume continuous data movement, low-latency processing, or durable ordered event streams are central requirements. |  |  |
| Direct integration | The integration is intentionally local, constrained, low reuse, or temporary, and the exception from reusable capability design is documented. |  |  |

h2. Architecture diagram

Insert a high-level diagram aligned with the target architecture.

h2. Architecture decision record (ADR)

Title: [Describe capability]
Status: Proposed / Approved

Context:
* Key business requirements
* Technical constraints
* Expected volumes / performance

Decision:
* Chosen integration pattern and why
* Reuse, if any, chosen and why

Alternatives considered:
* Rejected approaches and reasons

Consequences:
* Pros, cons, risks, mitigations

Follow-up actions:
* Register APIs, event topics, data products, file contracts, governance checks, or support model updates

de.body:
# Automation Cycle

A cycle for identifying, designing, delivering, enabling, and improving automation opportunities.

Purpose: Productize automation opportunities into governed workflows with clear users, platforms, controls, delivery paths, enablement, and monitoring.
Audience: automation-owner, process-owner, automation-engineer, platform-owner

## Route

Automation Opportunity Strategy -> Process/User Requirements -> Automation Platform Decision -> Automation Workflow Design -> Automation Delivery -> Automation Readiness Review -> Automation Enablement -> Automation Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Automation Opportunity Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: Welche Reise eines Kunden oder Partners soll mit der API unterstützt werden?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kunde erkennt Bedarf | Wie erkennt der Kunde oder Nutzer ein Bedürfnis oder Problem? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Wer ist der typische Kunde oder Nutzer, der diese Reise erlebt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Probleme | Was sind die Probleme oder Herausforderungen des Kunden? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Schritte durchläuft der Kunde bei der Reise? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Kundenbedürfnis wird gelöst | Wie wird das Bedürfnis des Kunden letztendlich gelöst? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Nutzen | Welchen Gewinn oder Nutzen hat der Kunde? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Inputs & Outputs | Was sind die Inputs und Outputs bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln | Wie lauten die Interaktions- und Verarbeitungsregeln bei jedem Schritt? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Automation Opportunity Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: Was sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und Geschäftsregeln im Zusammenhang mit der API?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ausgewählte Customer Journey Schritte | Welche Customer Journey Schritte sind für diese Domäne relevant? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Grundlegende Entitäten & geschäftliche Bedeutung | Welches sind die wesentlichen Entitäten und ihre Bedeutung für das Geschäft? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Attribute & geschäftliche Wichtigkeit | Welches sind die Schlüsselattribute der einzelnen Entitäten und ihre geschäftliche Wichtigkeit? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Beziehungen zwischen Entitäten | Welche Beziehungen bestehen zwischen den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln | Welche Geschäfts-, Compliance- und Integritätsregeln gelten für die Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Überlegungen zu Sicherheit und Datenschutz | Welche Überlegungen zur Sicherheit und zum Datenschutz gibt es im Zusammenhang mit den Entitäten? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Process/User Requirements - Consumer Experience Requirements Canvas

Purpose: What experience and non-functional requirements do consumers need before deciding the best integration architecture?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=consumerExperienceRequirementsCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer goals | What are the consumer's business goals, workflow goals, decision goals, automation goals, or data usage goals? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Availability and timeliness | When must the capability be available, how fresh must information be, and what latency or delivery windows matter? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Volume and performance | What request, event, record, file, batch, user, or transaction volumes must the capability support now and later? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Data quality and consistency | What accuracy, completeness, consistency, ordering, deduplication, reconciliation, or validation expectations do consumers have? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Security, privacy, and compliance | What identity, authorization, confidentiality, residency, consent, retention, audit, or regulatory constraints apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Automation Workflow Design - Interaction Canvas

Purpose: Welche Arten von Interaktionen sollte diese API unterstützen, bevor Sie sich für ein protokollspezifisches Design entscheiden?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=interactionCanvas&locale=de

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CRUD Interaktionen | Sind hier Interaktionen im CRUD-Stil erforderlich? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| CRUD Input- & Output-Modelle | Was sind die Eingabe- und Ausgabemodelle für die CRUD-Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil benötigt wird? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| CRUD Verarbeitung & Validierung | Was sind die Verarbeitungs- und Validierungsregeln für die CRUD-Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil benötigt wird? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Abfragegesteuerte Interaktionen | Welche Lese- oder Abfrageinteraktionen sind erforderlich, um Verbraucherfragen zu beantworten? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Abfragegesteuerte Input- & Output-Modelle | Was sind die Eingabe- und Ausgabemodelle für die abfragegesteuerten Interaktionen? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Abfragegesteuerte Verarbeitung & Validierung | Was sind die Verarbeitungs- und Validierungsregeln für die abfragegesteuerten Interaktionen? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Befehlsgesteuerte Interaktionen | Welche Befehle zur Zustandsänderung sind gegebenenfalls erforderlich? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Befehlsgesteuerte Input- & Output-Modelle | Was sind die Eingabe- und Ausgabemodelle für die befehlsgesteuerten Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil benötigt wird? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Befehlsgesteuerte Verarbeitung & Validierung | Was sind die Verarbeitungs- und Validierungsregeln für die befehlsgesteuerten Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil benötigt wird? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Eventgesteuerte Interaktionen | Welche Ereignisse müssen gegebenenfalls veröffentlicht oder verarbeitet werden? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Eventgesteuerte Input- & Output-Modelle | Was sind die Eingabe- und Ausgabemodelle für die ereignisgesteuerten Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil erforderlich ist? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |
| Eventgesteuerte Verarbeitung & Validierung | Was sind die Verarbeitungs- und Validierungsregeln für die ereignisgesteuerten Interaktionen, falls dieser Stil benötigt wird? | integration-architect |  |  |  |  |

sections.title: Intent & Fit
sections.description: Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

sections.title: Requirements
sections.description: Gather expert input using the recommended questions. Treat answers as inputs to design decisions, not final design decisions.

sections.title: Architecture Decision
sections.description: Compare viable architecture choices against the gathered requirements, evidence, risks, and open questions.

sections.title: Architecture Decision Record
sections.description: Record the selected decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences, evidence, and follow-up actions.

pt.body:
# Capability Productization Cycle

A cycle for turning business capabilities into reusable digital capabilities before selecting the implementation style.

Purpose: Identify and productize reusable digital capabilities before deciding whether the delivery style should be APIs, events, files, streams, data products, AI-enabled services, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.
Audience: business-owner, platform-architect, capability-owner

## Route

Strategy -> Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Architecture & Platform Decisions -> Solution & Interface Design -> Delivery & Operations -> Quality & Readiness Assurance -> Publishing & Enablement -> Monitoring & Improvement

## Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

### Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: Qual é a jornada do cliente ou parceiro que a API pretende apoiar?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=pt

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| O cliente descobre a necessidade | Como o cliente reconhece sua necessidade ou problema? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Persona | Quem é o cliente típico que passa por essa jornada? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Dores | Quais são os pontos fracos ou desafios do cliente? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Etapas da jornada | Quais são as etapas que o cliente percorre em sua jornada? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Necessidade do cliente resolvida | Como a necessidade do cliente é resolvida no final? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Ganhos | Quais são os ganhos ou benefícios do cliente? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Entradas e saídas | Quais são as entradas e saídas em cada etapa? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Regras de interação e processamento | Quais são as regras de interação e processamento em cada etapa? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Domain Canvas

Purpose: Quais são as entidades principais e as regras de negócios relacionadas à API?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=domainCanvas&locale=pt

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Etapas selecionadas da jornada do cliente | Quais etapas da jornada do cliente são relevantes para este domínio? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Entidades principais e significado comercial | Quais são as entidades principais e seu significado comercial? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Atributos e importância comercial | Quais são os principais atributos de cada entidade e sua importância comercial? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Relações entre entidades | Quais são as relações entre as entidades? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Regras de negócios, conformidade e integridade | Quais são as regras de negócios, conformidade e integridade relacionadas às entidades? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Considerações sobre segurança e privacidade | Quais são as considerações sobre segurança e privacidade relacionadas às entidades? | domain-specialist |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Capability Value Proposition Canvas

Purpose: Which reusable capability would create value for consumers without deciding yet whether it should be delivered as an API, event, file, stream, data product, or another integration style?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityValuePropositionCanvas&locale=pt

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Consumer tasks and outcomes | What are consumers, partners, users, systems, or teams trying to achieve? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Gain-enabling capability features | What capability features would help consumers achieve better outcomes, speed, automation, insight, reach, or compliance? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Pain-relieving capability features | What capability features would remove friction, manual work, errors, delays, risk, or uncertainty for consumers? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Reusable capabilities | What reusable business or data capabilities could serve these tasks, gains, and pains across more than one consumer or use case? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

### Strategy - Capability Business Model Canvas

Purpose: How viable, reusable, funded, owned, supported, and discoverable should this integration capability be?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=capabilityBusinessModelCanvas&locale=pt

| Topic | Question | Suggested answer owner | Answer | Confidence | Evidence | Open questions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Key partners | Which business, technology, data, security, legal, platform, or external partners are needed to make the capability work? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key activities | What must the capability owner and producers do to design, deliver, govern, support, and improve the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Key resources | Which systems, data assets, platforms, people, standards, funding, and operational capabilities are required? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability value proposition | What value does this reusable capability provide to consumers and to the organization or ecosystem? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Consumer engagement | How will consumers discover, evaluate, request, onboard, get support for, and provide feedback on the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Channels | Through which catalogs, portals, marketplaces, documentation sites, support paths, or governance processes will consumers interact with the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Capability consumer segments | Who are the current and potential consumers of the capability, including teams, partners, systems, products, or data users? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Costs | What are the significant costs of building, operating, governing, supporting, and evolving the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |
| Benefits | What business, operational, ecosystem, reuse, compliance, or cost benefits justify the capability? | capability-owner |  |  |  |  |

| Onboarding and access | How should consumers find, request, test, get approved for, and start using the capability? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Change and versioning | How much change tolerance do consumers have, and what notice, compatibility, migration, or versioning expectations apply? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Observability and support | What monitoring, status, traceability, data quality visibility, support, ownership, and incident communication do consumers need? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Recovery and continuity | What replay, retry, reconciliation, backup, fallback, continuity, or manual recovery expectations must be supported? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |
| Architecture implications | What do these requirements imply for possible architecture styles, such as APIs, events, files, streams, data products, or direct integration? | api-consumer-specialist |  |  |  |  |

CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=businessImpactCanvas&locale=pt

| Características de localização / zona | Quais são as características dessas localizações ou zonas, tais como propriedade, região ou exposição? | platform-architect |  |  |  |  |

pt.body:
h1. Capability Productization Cycle

A cycle for turning business capabilities into reusable digital capabilities before selecting the implementation style.

Purpose: Identify and productize reusable digital capabilities before deciding whether the delivery style should be APIs, events, files, streams, data products, AI-enabled services, direct integration, or a hybrid pattern.
Audience: business-owner, platform-architect, capability-owner

h2. Route

Strategy -> Consumer Requirements & Onboarding -> Architecture & Platform Decisions -> Solution & Interface Design -> Delivery & Operations -> Quality & Readiness Assurance -> Publishing & Enablement -> Monitoring & Improvement

h2. Intent & Fit

Capture the business outcome, consumers, producers, scope, constraints, and decision status.

h2. Requirements

Gather expert input using the recommended questions. Treat answers as inputs to design decisions, not final design decisions.

h2. Architecture Decision

Compare viable architecture choices against the gathered requirements, evidence, risks, and open questions.

h2. Architecture Decision Record

Record the selected decision, rationale, alternatives, consequences, evidence, and follow-up actions.

h2. Question List

Expert answers are inputs to architecture and design decisions, not final design decisions. Use evidence, confidence, and open questions to keep assumptions visible.

h3. Strategy - Customer Journey Canvas

Purpose: Qual é a jornada do cliente ou parceiro que a API pretende apoiar?
CanvasCreator: https://canvascreator.apiopscycles.com/?canvas=customerJourneyCanvas&locale=pt

|| Topic || Question || Suggested answer owner || Answer || Confidence || Evidence || Open questions ||
| O cliente descobre a necessidade | Como o cliente reconhece sua necessidade ou problema? | business-owner |  |  |  |  |
